danger_signals.visual_form: Animated bullet sprites moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; contact causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctly non-human sprite designs with aggressive animations
Black or dark-shaded void areas at stage edges and floor gaps
Significantly larger sprite entities with unique designs, placed at predetermined encounter points
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring sustained engagement and pattern recognition

danger_signals.visual_form: Moving tiles and objects rendered via background layer or sprite animation
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger requiring timing and navigation skill

safety_signals.visual_form: Flying pods descending with glowing weapon icons (homing missiles, torpedoes, flamethrower, laser, spread shot)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary weapon upgrade; increased firepower and tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Temporary protective aura rendered around player character
Explosive ordnance icon dropped or collected
safety_signals.meaning: Screen-wide damage capability; crowd control resource

safety_signals.visual_form: Platform tiles with defined edges, clearly rendered surface geometry
safety_signals.meaning: Traversable space; no immediate fall risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Upright stance, weapon readiness pose vs. falling or hit animation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Green and flesh tones (original sprite design)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military uniform silhouette; machine gun armaments
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Replaced with robot designs (RD-008, RC-011) in European 'Super Probotector' version to comply with German violence regulations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases, urban environments, industrial settings as stages

faction_indicators.color_identity: Purple, pink, cyan non-natural color schemes contrasting with human environments
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Biomechanical and organic alien creature designs; distinctly extraterrestrial morphology
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple creature types with unique silhouettes (flying pods, ground units, boss entities with distinct visual identities)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien structures, corrupted landscapes, invading mothership/brain entity appearances in cutscenes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stages feature integrated alien structures and corrupted Earth environments; sky and atmosphere show signs of extraterrestrial presence; intro cutscenes display battle scenarios with explosions and combat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies are hostile alien units; NPCs (in Japanese version) react with aggressive dialogue; player must defend against continuous alien assault across six themed stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Early stages show urban and industrial Earth locations being invaded; later stages transition to alien territory and mothership interiors; visual contamination increases as player advances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density and sophistication increases; boss encounters become more elaborate; narrative progression indicated through cutscene visuals showing escalating threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss defeat triggers visual feedback (explosion, dissolve, status change); stage clear screen with score and performance metrics; transition cutscene to next stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All stage enemies cease spawning; player gains temporary invulnerability during transition; difficulty and enemy composition changes for subsequent stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: End-game message screens differ by difficulty; Japanese version displays ending image montage timestamps; hard mode features enhanced celebration screen with bonus NPCs and environmental details
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Japanese version provides final boss access on Normal+ difficulty only; international versions restrict credits visibility; enemy AI aggression and projectile patterns scale with difficulty

environmental_storytelling.example: Stages transition from Earth military/urban environments to alien mothership interiors, visually communicating player advancement from defending home territory to attacking enemy stronghold

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy and boss visual design hierarchy
environmental_storytelling.example: Small alien creatures appear in early/mid stages; mid-stage bosses are oversized variants; final boss (brain entity) is grotesquely alien and architecturally integrated into stage, signaling culmination of invasion threat

Japanese version: animated human characters with color-coded text and explicit aggressive dialogue; European version: robot replacements (Probotectors) with unanimated sprites, same spatial staging but altered narrative tone regarding violence
Flying pods with weapon icons communicate tactical options without text; weapon variety (flamethrower, laser, spread shot) visually distinct allows player to anticipate combat capability changes

Two stages use Mode 7 rendering with rotatable screen space; visual shift from side-view communicates environmental variety and stage design innovation, suggests non-linear navigation and target-finding objectives distinct from linear platforming

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Player character (Bill Rizer/Lance Bean or Probotector)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Upright stance with weapon readiness; dual machine gun configuration visible; responds to input with jumping and directional firing
Knockback animation; temporary invulnerability flash; weapon downgrade if power-up was equipped; life count decrements
Aerial somersault with dual-gun fire in circular pattern; both weapons at risk of loss if damaged during move; visual spectacle communicating high-risk/high-reward action
Static or looping movement pattern; positioned at stage waypoints; minimal projectile fire until player proximity
Increased projectile rate; aggressive movement toward player; animation speed increases; visual warning signals imminent damage threat
Animation speed increases; projectile patterns become denser or erratic; visual effects intensify (flashing, discoloration); arena may shift or transform
Animated descent from top of screen; glowing icon visible; collision with player triggers weapon swap and automatic pickup
Transparent or semi-opaque protective aura rendered around player; visual distinctness from normal state signals temporary invulnerability window

notes: Contra III employs dual visual communication strategies: (1) direct threat signaling through enemy sprite design, projectile animations, and environmental hazards; (2) regional localization affecting narrative tone—human commandos in Japanese/US versions vs. robots in European version fundamentally alters character-to-player identification without changing mechanical danger communication. Overhead perspective stages (Mode 7) visually distinguish navigation objectives from combat-focused side-scrolling sections. Difficulty modes are communicated through end-game narrative messaging and hidden debug codes reflecting Japanese arcade tradition (Street Fighter button combinations). Power-up system uses icon-based visual language reducing text dependency. Boss design scales visually to suggest threat escalation and invasion progression.
